summary: Acoustic Cassidy shines on a modest four‑song set
blurb: The program runs just four songs across two short sets. The acoustic rendition of "Cassidy" is the clear highlight, with the band delivering solid, concise performances throughout.
review: Recorded at NBC Studios on May 7, 1981, this brief Dead outing clocks in at only four numbers – “On The Road Again,” “Dire Wolf,” “Deep Elem Blues” and an acoustic “Cassidy.” While the setlist is sparse, reviewers note that the stripped‑down version of "Cassidy" is an incredible performance that rises above the rest of the material. Jerry’s lead work and Brent’s keyboards stay tight, but no other moments receive particular praise in the available commentary. The sole source, a TV/VHS capture (gd1981-05-07.147903.tv.vhs.flac), is described as fair in quality; it delivers enough clarity to enjoy the standout acoustic moment, though listeners should temper expectations regarding overall sonic fidelity.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Only one recording analysis is available, and its sentiment is positive, highlighting an "incredible" acoustic performance of "Cassidy." The limited data warrants a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Only known source; captures the praised acoustic "Cassidy" despite overall fair quality.

key_highlights: Acoustic rendition of "Cassidy" stands out as an incredible performance.
Short, concise four‑song set offers tight playing throughout.
Recording is fair but sufficient to hear the standout moment.
